revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Chiryu Shrine | image=File:Chiryu Jinja Haiden.jpg | location={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|qid=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Chiryū|en|Chiryū|qid=Q223496|lt=Chiryu|1=Chiryū}}, Nishimachi Kanda 12 | coordinates={{Coord|35.013136|N|137.041111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity={{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|qid=Q1373935|1=Ugayafukiaezu}} | Shrine_Rank=[[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha]] (small)
[[Mikawa Province|Mikawa Province]] [[Ninomiya|Second Shrine]]
Former [[Prefectural Shrine]] | founded=(Traditional) Reign of Emperor Keikō (12th Emperor) | Main_hall=Three-bay Nagare-zukuri, thatched with cypress bark
(The shrine buildings are in the Owari-zukuri style) | Other_names=Ikeshiruhime Daimyōjin | Sacred_sites=One of the Three Shrines of the Tōkaidō | annual_festival=[[May 3]] (Chiryu festival) | Cultural_properties=Pagoda (Important Cultural Property) and others | map = {{location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=35|lat_min=0|lat_sec=47.2|lon_deg=137|lon_min=2|lon_sec=28|label=Chiryu Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|qq=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|0|47.2|N|137|2|28|E|qq=}}|qq=}} | name=Chiryu Shrine | location={{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|qid=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Chiryū|en|Chiryū|qid=Q223496|lt=Chiryu|1=Chiryū}}, Nishimachi Kanda 12 | Sacred_objects= | Shrine_Rank={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}（small）
{{ill|Mikawa Province|en|Mikawa Province|qid=Q768158|lt=Mikawa Province|1=Mikawa Province}} {{ill|Ichinomiya|en|Ichinomiya|qid=Q1656379|lt=Ichinomiya|1=Ichinomiya}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded=Traditionally during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}} (12th emperor) | Main_hall=Three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with hinoki bark roofing
(Shrine buildings are in the Owari style) | alternate_name=Ikerifuno Daimyojin | Pilgrimage_route=One of the three shrines along the Tokaido | annual_festival={{ill|May 3|en|May 3|qid=Q2547|lt=May 3|1=May 3}} ({{ill|Chiryu festival|ja|知立まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiryu festival|qq=}}) | Rituals= | Cultural_properties=Tahoto Pagoda (Important Cultural Property) and others| map={{location map|Japan Aichi|lat_deg=35|lat_min=0|lat_sec=47.2|lon_deg=137|lon_min=2|lon_sec=28|label=Chiryu Shrine|position=top|width=200|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|qq=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|0|47.2|N|137|2|28|E|qq=}}|qq=}} | prefecture={{ill|Nishi-machi|qid=Q56856752|1=Nishi-machi}} | architecture_style={{ill|Owari-zukuri|qid=Q11465322|1=Owari-zukuri}} }} [[File:Chiryu-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|Torii]] '''Chiryu Shrine''' (知立神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|qid=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Chiryū|en|Chiryū|qid=Q223496|lt=Chiryu|1=Chiryū}}, Nishimachi. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, a {{ill|Ninomiya|en|Ninomiya|qid=Q841389|lt=Ninomiya|1=Ninomiya}} of {{ill|Mikawa Province|en|Mikawa Province|qid=Q768158|lt=Mikawa Province|1=Mikawa Province}}, and its former shrine Rank is {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. The former name of the shrine is "Ikerifuno Daimyojin." During the Edo period, it was counted as one of the "Three Shrines of the Tokaido."[[File:Chiryu-jinja torii.JPG|thumb|Torii]] '''Chiryu Shrine''' (知立神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Chiryū|en|Chiryū|qid=Q223496|lt=Chiryu|1=Chiryū}}, {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|qid=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, Nishimachi. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and the second shrine of {{ill|Mikawa Province|en|Mikawa Province|qid=Q768158|lt=Mikawa Province|1=Mikawa Province}}. The former shrine Rank is Prefectural Shrine. The old name is "Ikeshiruhime Daimyōjin". In the Edo period, it was counted as one of the "Three Shrines of the Tōkaidō". == Deities ==
'''deity Genealogy''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"|qq=}} {{familytree|border=0|01|y|02| 01=[[Hoori|Hikohohodemi no Mikoto]]|02={{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|qid=Q2329261|lt={{color|#FC4E6B|Toyotama-hime no Mikoto|qq=}}}}
（Chinbo Shrine）|qq=}} {{familytree|border=0||||!||qq=}} {{familytree|border=0||||01|y|02| 01='''[[Ugayafukiaezu|Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto]]'''|02={{ill|Tamayori-hime|en|Tamayori-hime|qid=Q56344982|lt={{color|#FC4E6B|Tamayorihime no Mikoto|qq=}}}}|qq=}} {{familytree|border=0|||||||!||qq=}} {{familytree|border=0|||||01| 01=Kamuyamato Iwarebiko no Mikoto
（Emperor Jimmu）|qq=}} {{familytree/end|qq=}}
== Deities == The enshrined deities are the following six pillars. The four main deities are enshrined together with two additional deities in the associated shrine.Shrine history book. ; Main deities :* {{ill|Ugayafukiaezu|en|Ugayafukiaezu|qid=Q1373935|lt=Ugayafukiaezu|1=Ugayafukiaezu}} （Main deity） :* {{ill|Hoori|en|Hoori|qid=Q1051960|lt=Hoori|1=Hoori}} （Father of Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto） :* {{ill|Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|en|Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|qid=Q3082751|lt=Tamayori-hime|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)}} （Wife of Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto） :* Kamuyamato Iwarebiko no Mikoto ({{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}) (Child of Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto) ; Associated deities :* Aomi no Obito no Mikoto (A person who is said to have pioneered the Hekikai region) :* {{ill|Prince Shōtoku|en|Prince Shōtoku|qid=Q263972|lt=Prince Shōtoku|1=Prince Shōtoku}} In literature, there are theories that the deities are {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}'s followers {{ill|Otomo no Takehiko|ja|大伴武日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otomo no Takehiko|qq=}} and {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}}, or that they are derived from the name "Chiryu," linking them to {{ill|Konohanachiru-hime|en|Konohanachiru-hime|qid=Q48745675|lt=Konohana Chiru-hime|1=Konohanachiru-hime}}. == History == === Foundation === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was founded when {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}}, who went to subdue the eastern provinces during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, prayed for victory here and enshrined the four deities of the nation's founders upon his victorious return. Another theory places its foundation in the first year of {{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|qid=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}}'s reign. According to the family records of the Nagami clan, who worshipped this shrine as their guardian shrine until the Edo period, they are descendants of {{ill|Nigi Hayahi|ja|ニギハヤヒ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigi Hayahi|qq=}}, and their ancestor, Mikawa no Suke Teiren, became the shrine priest by imperial command of {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in 673. Initially, the shrine was said to be located about 1 km to the east in Yamamachi. It temporarily moved to Kamishigehara before relocating to its current location in 1573. == Overview ==
{| class="wikitable" style="font-size:85%;background-color:#ffffff;text-align:center;white-space: nowrap" |+The Rank transitions of the three main gods of Mikawa !Year!!{{ill|Toga Shrine|en|Toga Shrine|qid=Q3530344|lt=Toga Shrine|1=Toga Shrine}}!!Chiryu Shrine!!{{ill|Sanage Shrine|ja|猿投神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanage Shrine|qq=}} |- |850||Junior Fifth Rank, Lower|| --||-- |- |851||Junior Fifth Rank, Upper||Junior Fifth Rank, Upper||Junior Fifth Rank, Lower |- |864||Junior Fifth Rank, Upper
→ Senior Fifth Rank, Lower||Junior Fifth Rank, Upper
→ Senior Fifth Rank, Lower||Junior Fifth Rank, Lower
→ Junior Fifth Rank, Upper |- |870||Senior Fifth Rank, Lower
→ Senior Fifth Rank, Upper||Senior Fifth Rank, Lower
→ Senior Fifth Rank, Upper||Junior Fifth Rank, Upper
→ Senior Fifth Rank, Lower |- |876||Junior Fourth Rank, Lower
→ Junior Fourth Rank, Upper||Junior Fourth Rank, Lower
→ Junior Fourth Rank, Upper||Junior Fifth Rank, Upper
→ Senior Fifth Rank, Lower |- |877||--||--||Senior Fifth Rank, Upper
→ Junior Fourth Rank, Lower |- |Ichinomiya system||Ichinomiya||Ninomiya||Sannomiya |}
According to {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|qid=Q1151168|lt=Six National Histories|1=Rikkokushi}}, the deity Chiryu (Chiryu) was promoted to Junior Fifth Rank, Upper in 851, Senior Fifth Rank, Lower in 864, Senior Fifth Rank, Upper in 870, and Junior Fourth Rank, Upper in 876. It is recorded alongside {{ill|Toga Shrine|en|Toga Shrine|qid=Q3530344|lt=Toga Shrine|1=Toga Shrine}} in each article, and by the end of the Six National Histories, its Rank was among the highest in Mikawa Province alongside Toga Shrine.{{Cite book|和書|editor=谷川健一編|author=野本欽也|year=1987|chapter=Chiryu Shrine|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 10 東海|publisher=白水社|isbn=4560022208|ref=知立神社（神々）|1987th year |qq=}} In 927, the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} lists Chiryu Shrine in Hekikai District, Mikawa Province. According to the {{ill|Wamyō Ruijushō|ja|和名抄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wamyō Ruijushō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 和名類聚抄}}, there is a "Chiryu no Sato" in Hekikai District, which is believed to be this area. The {{ill|Sankai Meisho Zue|ja|参河国名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankai Meisho Zue|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} notes that Chiryu Shrine was regarded as the Ninomiya of Mikawa Province, but no medieval documents explicitly record it as such.{{Cite book|和書|editor=中世諸国一宮制研究会|author=| year=2000|chapter=|title=中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究|publisher=岩田書院|isbn=978-4872941708|ref=中世諸国一宮制|2000th year |p=125-127|qq=}} During the Middle Ages, the shrine was revered by the Mizuno clan. In 1471, Mizuno Tadanao rebuilt the shrine buildings, and Mizuno Tadamasa repaired them in 1526, but the buildings were burned down during the 1547 conflict, leading to the shrine's relocation to Shigehara. It moved to its current location in 1571, with repairs and land donations from Mizuno Nobumitsu. In the Edo period, Matsudaira Tadayasu donated an additional 10 koku of shrine land during the Kanbun era (1661–1672). Known primarily as Ikerifuno Daimyojin, the shrine was one of the "Three Shrines of the Tokaido," revered as a local guardian deity by more than 20 nearby villages, and believed to protect against snake bites, prevent long worms, ensure safe childbirth, and bring rain. Amulets from the shrine were believed to protect against snake bites, leading to branches being established from northern Kanto to the San'in region. The {{ill|Tokaido Meisho Zue|ja|東海道名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokaido Meisho Zue|qq=}} describes the shrine's deities, Tahoto pagoda, old plaques, branch shrines, sacred gates, stone bridges, archery ranges, snake-repelling amulets, and sacred washing ponds.Waseda University "Classics Comprehensive Database," {{ill|Tokaido Meisho Zue Volume 3|ja|東海道名所図会 第3巻|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokaido Meisho Zue Volume 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: [https://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/bunko30/bunko30_e0205/bunko30_e0205_0003/bunko30_e0205_0003_p0028.jpg 28 cut], [https://archive.wul.waseda.ac.jp/kosho/bunko30/bunko30_e0205/bunko30_e0205_0003/bunko30_e0205_0003_p0029.jpg 29 cut] After the Meiji Restoration, the shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1872 under the modern shrine Rank system. === Shrine Rank === * Records of shrine Rank promotions during the Six National Histories period ** October 7, 851: Junior Fifth Rank, Upper ({{ill|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052905|lt=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Montoku Tennō Jitsuroku}}) - recorded as "Chiryu Kami." ** February 19, 864: Promoted from Junior Fifth Rank, Upper to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) - recorded as "Chiryu Kami." ** August 28, 870: Promoted from Senior Fifth Rank, Lower to Senior Fifth Rank, Upper ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) - recorded as "Chiryu Kami." ** June 8, 876: Promoted from Junior Fourth Rank, Lower to Junior Fourth Rank, Upper ({{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}) - recorded as "Chiryu Kami." * After the Six National Histories ** Senior First Rank (1301: plaque in the shrine's collection) - recorded as "Chiryu Daimyojin." ** Senior First Rank (1649: {{ill|Mikawa Domestic Shrine name Register|ja|三河国内神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikawa Domestic Shrine name Register|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) - recorded as "Korin Daimyojin." *: Shrine tradition holds that the Rank was promoted to Senior First Rank on February 20, 1261. == Grounds == [[File:Chiryu Jinja 2020-04 ac (1).jpg|thumb|Haiden (Registered Tangible Cultural Property)]] [[File:Chiryu Jinja 2020-04 ac (5).jpg|thumb|right|{{ill|Tahōtō|en|Tahōtō|qid=Q2451601|lt=Tahōtō|1=Tahōtō}} (Important Cultural Property)]] The current main shrine buildings date from the late Edo period to the Showa era. The shrine layout is called "'''{{ill|Owari-zukuri|ja|尾張造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Owari-zukuri|qq=}}'''", with the main hall, offering hall, ritual hall, corridor, and front shrine connected in a long line. This style is common in Owari Province but rare in Mikawa Province, and the Chiryu Shrine buildings are significant as examples of its spread into Mikawa.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Chiryu Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref=明治神社誌料|1912nd year |qq=}} These shrine buildings are registered as Tangible Cultural Properties. Details of each building are as follows: :; Main hall :: Built in 1831 during the Edo period. It is a three-bay {{ill|Nagare-zukuri|en|Nagare-zukuri|qid=Q210684|lt=Nagare-zukuri|1=Nagare-zukuri}} with a hinoki bark roof.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Chiryu Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref=明治神社誌料|1912nd year |qq=}} :; Offering hall :: Connected in front of the main hall. Built in the Taisho era (1912–1926). It is five bays wide and three bays deep, with a gabled roof and hinoki bark roofing.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Chiryu Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref=明治神社誌料|1912nd year |qq=}} :; Ritual hall and corridor :: The ritual hall is connected in front of the offering hall, with corridors extending to the left and right. Built in 1887, it was renovated in the Taisho and Showa eras. The ritual hall has a four-pillar gate style with a gabled roof. Both the ritual hall and the corridors have hinoki bark roofs.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Chiryu Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref=明治神社誌料|1912nd year |qq=}} :; Front shrine :: Located at the front of the main shrine complex, connected to the ritual hall. It is six bays wide and three bays deep, with a gabled roof and hinoki bark roofing. The front two bays have earthen floors, while the back has wooden floors. There is a one-bay-wide gabled porch attached to the front.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Chiryu Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref=明治神社誌料|1912nd year |qq=}} In addition, the '''stone bridge''' over the sacred pond in front of the front shrine was built in 1732 during the Edo period. Made of granite, it is a drum bridge measuring 6.6 meters long and 2.4 meters wide. The arched stone beams are topped with 12 cm thick stone slabs to form the bridge. It is mentioned in the {{ill|Tokaido Meisho Zue|ja|東海道名所図会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokaido Meisho Zue|qq=}}. This stone bridge is designated as a cultural property of Chiryu City.Information board in the shrine grounds. At the entrance to the grounds stands a '''{{ill|Tahōtō|en|Tahōtō|qid=Q2451601|lt=Tahōtō|1=Tahōtō}}''', which is believed to have been rebuilt in 1509 during the late Muromachi period. It is a two-story, 14.5-meter-tall tower with a cypress bark roof and ornamental finials at each corner. According to shrine tradition, Ennin (Jikaku Daishi) built a two-story pagoda in 850, but the current structure is a later reconstruction. The main deity (Aizen Myo-o) and the altar were removed from the tower during the Haibutsu Kishaku (abolition of Buddhism) movement in 1868, which saved it from destruction. The tower was restored during repairs in 1920. The main deity that was inside the tower is now enshrined at {{ill|Sōjiji (Ch iryu)|ja|総持寺 (知立市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōjiji (Ch iryu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This Tahoto is designated as an Important Cultural Property of Japan.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/yukei/kenzoubutu/kunisitei/0010.html |title=Chiryu Shrine Tahoto |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |accessdate=2014-11-16|qq=}}Information board in the shrine grounds. Other notable features within the grounds include a tea house called "Ikerifuno-an" (a Registered Tangible Cultural Property) relocated from a local old house in 1968, a Western-style building called "Yoseikan" built in 1886, and a 6-meter-tall {{ill|Fraxinus|ja|トネリコ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fraxinus|qq=}} tree (designated a Natural Monument of Chiryu City).{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Chiryu Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref=明治神社誌料|1912nd year |qq=}}Information board in the shrine grounds. File:Chiryu Jinja 2020-04 ac (2).jpg|Stone bridge (Cultural Property of Chiryu City) File:Chiryu-jinja toneriko.JPG|{{ill|Fraxinus|ja|トネリコ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fraxinus|qq=}} (Natural Monument of Chiryu City) == Associated Shrines == [[File:Chiryu-jinja Ubagami-sha.JPG|thumb|Registered Tangible Cultural Property Ubagami Shrine]] [[File:Chiryu-jinja Tsuchinogozen-sha.JPG|thumb|Tsuchinogozen Shrine]] ; Associated shrines * Ubagami Shrine ** Enshrined deity: {{ill|Toyotama-hime|en|Toyotama-hime|qid=Q2329261|qid=Q2329261|lt=Toyotama-hime|1=Toyotama-hime}} (Mother of Ugayafukiaezu no Mikoto). The shrine building is a one-bay Nagare-zukuri structure with vermilion lacquer and a hinoki bark roof. Built in the Meiji period, it is large for an associated shrine. The building is registered as a Tangible Cultural Property.{{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Chiryu Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref=明治神社誌料|1912nd year |qq=}} * Tsuchinogozen Shrine ** Enshrined deity: {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|ja|吉備武彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Takehiko|qq=}} (A figure who is said to have been involved in the founding of Chiryu Shrine) ; Branch shrines * Goshiden - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} and others. Built in 1873 following the shrine consolidation order, it enshrines small shrines from within and outside the grounds. * Koyama Ten Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少彦名命|1=Sukunabikona|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}. * Akiba Shrine - Enshrined deity: {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|en|Kagu-tsuchi|qid=Q1640658|lt=Kagutsuchi|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}. Relocated to the grounds of Chiryu Shrine in 1961. File:Chiryu-jinja Goushi-den.JPG|Goshiden File:Chiryu-jinja Koyama-ten-jinja.JPG|Koyama Ten Shrine File:Chiryu-jinja Akiba-sha.JPG|Akiba Shrine == festivals == [[File:Tiryuumatsuri7.JPG|thumb|Floats and Tahoto during the Chiryu festival]] festivals are as follows:[http://chiryu-jinja.com/contents-3.html annual Events] Chiryu Shrine official website * Monthly festival (3rd of each month) * 11 Year's festival and Traffic Safety Prayer festival (January 1) * Foundation festival (January 3) * Setsubun festival (February 3) * National Foundation festival (February 11) * Prayer festival (March 3) * Showa festival (April 29) * Kojo-sha festival and Evening festival (May 2) * '''festival''' ('''{{ill|Chiryu festival|ja|知立まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiryu festival|qq=}}''') and Procession festival (May 3) *: On the day before and the day of the festival, five floats are offered in the main festival, and five flower floats are offered in the interim festival, which takes place every other year. Chiryu's karakuri and Chiryu float bunraku are both designated as Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties of Japan.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/mukeiminzoku/kunisitei/0792.html |title=Chiryu's Float Bunraku and Karakuri |publisher=Aichi Prefecture |accessdate=2014-11-16|qq=}} For more details, see "Chiryu festival." * Iris festival (May 25 - June 20) * Grand Purification ceremony and Chinowa Ritual (July 31) * Tsuchinogozen Shrine festival (Late August Sunday) - Child Sumo dedication. * Akiba Shrine festival (September 20) - {{ill|Tezutsu-hanabi|en|Tezutsu-hanabi|qid=Q11497105|lt=Handheld fireworks|1=Tezutsu-hanabi}} dedication. * Meiji festival (November 3) * Shichi-Go-San Prayer festival (November 15) * Harvest festival (December 3) * Emperor's Birthday festival (December 23) * Grand Purification ceremony and 11 Year's Eve festival (December 31) == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * Tahoto Pagoda (Building) - Muromachi period. Designated on May 27, 1907.{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|1267|知立神社多宝塔|qq=}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties (Nationally Registered) === * main hall (Building) - Registered on April 25, 2014.{{国指定文化財等データベース|101|00009968|知立神社main hall|qq=}} * Offering Hall (Building) - Registered on April 25, 2014.{{国指定文化財等データベース|101|00009969|知立神社幣殿|qq=}} * Front Shrine (Building) - Registered on April 25, 2014.{{国指定文化財等データベース|101|00009970|知立神社拝殿|qq=}} * Ritual Hall and Corridor (Building) - Registered on April 25, 2014.{{国指定文化財等データベース|101|00009971|知立神社祭文殿及び回廊|qq=}} * Ubagami Shrine (Building) - Registered on April 25, 2014.{{国指定文化財等データベース|101|00009972|知立神社摂社親母神社|qq=}} * Tea House (Building) - Registered on April 25, 2014.{{国指定文化財等データベース|101|00009973|知立神社茶室|qq=}} === Cultural Properties Designated by Aichi Prefecture === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Plaque "Shoichi I Chiirifu Daimyojin" (Craft) - Kamakura period. Designated on January 12, 1957. ** Two Noh masks (Craft) - Muromachi period. Designated on October 14, 1964. ** Six bugaku masks (Craft) - Kamakura period. Designated on October 14, 1964. === Cultural Properties Designated by Chiryu City === * Tangible Cultural Properties ** Stone Bridge (Building) - Edo period. Designated on April 1, 1969. ** Lion Head (Craft) - Muromachi period. Designated on January 1, 1965. ** Wooden Frog (Craft) - Muromachi period. Designated on January 1, 1965. ** {{ill|Waniguchi|en|Waniguchi|qid=Q11674315|lt=Waniguchi|1=Waniguchi}} (Craft) - Edo period. Designated on January 1, 1965. ** Hōraikyō (Craft) - Muromachi period. Designated on January 1, 1965. ** Wooden Spear (Craft) - Muromachi period. Designated on January 1, 1965. ** {{ill|Handwarmer-shaped pottery|ja|手焙形土器|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Handwarmer-shaped pottery|qq=}} (Archaeological) - Yayoi period (Excavated near the shrine). Designated on January 1, 1965. ** Donation letter from {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} (Historical document) - Designated on January 1, 1965. ** Letter from Omanko of Chokaiin (Historical document) - Designated on January 1, 1965. ** Donation letter from {{ill|Matsudaira Kiyoyasu|en|Matsudaira Kiyoyasu|qid=Q1041434|lt=Matsudaira Kiyoyasu|1=Matsudaira Kiyoyasu}} (Historical document) - Designated on January 1, 1965. * Natural Monuments ** {{ill|Fraxinus|ja|トネリコ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fraxinus|qq=}} - Designated on June 10, 1982. == Jingu-ji (Temple associated with a shrine) == The {{ill|Jingū-ji|en|Jingū-ji|qid=Q1074971|lt=Jingu-ji|1=Jingū-ji}} that existed within the shrine grounds was said to have been established by the monk {{ill|Ennin|en|Ennin|qid=Q732282|lt=Ennin|1=Ennin}} (Jikaku Daishi) in 850. It originally had seven sub-temples, including Gyokusenbo, but they were destroyed by fire in 1547 and rebuilt during the Genki era (1570-1573). The existing Tahoto is a remnant of this Jingu-ji, with an inscription indicating it was rebuilt in 1509. During the Edo period, the temple was known as '''Sōjiji''', a sub-temple of {{ill|Kan'ei-ji|en|Kan'ei-ji|qid=Q3120766|lt=Kan'ei-ji|1=Kan'ei-ji}} and the administrative temple of Chiryu Shrine, but it was abolished in the Meiji era.{{Cite book|和書|editor=中世諸国一宮制研究会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究|publisher=岩田書院|isbn=978-4872941708|ref=中世諸国一宮制|2000th year |qq=}} The temple was later rebuilt near Chiryu Shrine in 1926. The main deity, Aizen Myo-o, which was housed in the Tahoto, is now enshrined at Sōjiji. == location Information == ; location * Nishimachi Kanda 12, Chiryu, Aichi Prefecture, Japan ; Access * Train: 10-minute walk from {{ill|Chiryū Station|en|Chiryū Station|qid=Q5102035|lt=Chiryu Station|1=Chiryū Station}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|en|Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|qid=Q1137838|lt=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} and {{ill|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|en|Meitetsu Mikawa Line|qid=Q11415758|lt=Meitetsu Mikawa Line|1=Meitetsu Mikawa Line}} ; Nearby * Remains of {{ill|Chiryū-juku|en|Chiryū-juku|qid=Q2963997|lt=Ikerifuno-juku|1=Chiryū-juku}} * {{ill|Sōjiji (Chiryu)|ja|総持寺 (知立市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sōjiji (Chiryu)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} - The successor temple to Chiryu Shrine's Jingu-ji. == References == {{Reflist|2|qq=}} == Further Reading == * Shrine history book * Information board in the shrine grounds * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=Chiryu Shrine|title=府県郷社明治神社誌料|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref=明治神社誌料|1912nd year |qq=}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/773|qq=}} '府県郷社明治神社誌料 上'] (National Diet Library Digital Collections) See 773-774 frames. * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Chiryu Shrine|title=日本歴史地名大系 23 愛知県の地名|publisher=平凡社|isbn=4582490239|ref=知立神社（平凡社）|1981st year |qq=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1989|chapter=Chiryu Shrine|title=角川日本地名大辞典 23 愛知県|publisher=角川書店|isbn=4040012305|ref=知立神社（角川）|1989th year |qq=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=谷川健一編|author=野本欽也|year=1987|chapter=Chiryu Shrine|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 10 東海|publisher=白水社|isbn=4560022208|ref=知立神社（神々）|1987th year |qq=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会編|author=野村辰美|chapter=Chiryu Shrine|year=1988|title=式内社調査報告 第9巻|publisher=皇學館大学出版部|page=|isbn=|ref=知立神社（式内社）|1988th year |qq=}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=中世諸国一宮制研究会|author=|year=2000|chapter=|title=中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究|publisher=岩田書院|isbn=978-4872941708|ref=中世諸国一宮制|2000th year |qq=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Miyaryu Kagura|ja|宮流神楽|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyaryu Kagura|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 熱田神楽・宮流神楽}} * {{ill|Chiryu festival|ja|知立まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiryu festival|qq=}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Chiryu-jinja|qq=}} * [http://chiryu-jinja.com/ Chiryu Shrine] - Official website * {{神道・神社史料集成|100201|qq=}} {{Shinto-navbox|qq=}} {{Normdaten|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|知立神社|version=99467280|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Chiryu-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] '''Chiryu Shrine (Q11584639)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Mikawa Province]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Hekikai District|en|Hekikai District|ja|碧海郡|ko|헤키카이군|pl|Powiat Hekikai|zh|碧海郡|lt=Hekikai district|qid=Q5368803|1=Hekikai District}} || {{ill|Chiryu Shrine|lt=Chiryū Shrine|ja|知立神社|commons|Category:Chiryu-jinja|qid=Q11584639|1=Chiryu Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || {{ill|Ni-no-Miya|qid=Q134917533|lt=Ni-no-Miya|1=Ni-no-Miya}} || — || {{coord|35.012765|137.040718|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181474 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11584639}}==Notes== [[da:d:Q11584639]] [[ja:知立神社]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Junior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mikawa Province]] [[Category:Ni-no-Miya]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:江戸時代の建築]] [[Category:知立市の建築物]] [[Category:知立市の歴史]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]]
